query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key takeaway for the forecasting question:

**The premise contains a critical timing error.** The Poland K2 tank "second executive contract" was already signed on **August 1, 2025** (~$6.5 billion) — not in the July–August 2026 window being asked about. As of July 10, 2026:

- **No confirmed >$5B single ROK arms export contract is publicly scheduled to sign in July–August 2026.**
- **Poland Orka submarines**: Lost to Sweden's Saab (June 29, 2026, ~$4.8B).
- **Canada submarines (CPSP)**: Lost to Germany's TKMS (July 7, 2026, ~$39B program).
- **K2 third executive contract**: Anticipated under the ~1,000-tank framework but with no confirmed date/value.
- **Middle East (UAE/Saudi/Iraq)**: Active Cheongung-II follow-on interest after strong combat performance, but no confirmed new >$5B contract.

A >$5B ROK deal in that specific window would require a *new, not-yet-announced* contract. Full structured findings with sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Poland doubles down on South Korean tanks with $6.5 billion deal
sources.snippet: Poland ordered 180 K2 Black Panther tanks with 81 vehicles from Hyundai Rotem, ~$6.5B, signed August 1, 2025 in Gliwice.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: Poland signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs on 1 August at Bumar-Łabędy, Gliwice.

sources.title: With $6.7 billion in new tanks and vehicles, it's 'Armor Week' in Poland
sources.snippet: Poland signed ~$6.7B in ground vehicle contracts, mostly the $6.5B Hyundai Rotem second tank deal.

sources.title: Poland signs agreement to produce South Korean K2 tanks domestically
sources.snippet: April 2026 Bumar-Łabędy–Hyundai Rotem agreement on local K2PL production.

sources.title: Poland signs $4.8B deal for three Saab A26 submarines
sources.snippet: Saab signed $4.83B contract with Poland June 29, 2026 for three A26 submarines under Orka; deliveries to 2038.

sources.title: Poland Signs SEK 47 Billion Contract for Three Saab A26 Submarines, Ending the Decade-Long Orka Tender
sources.snippet: On 29 June 2026 Poland signed with Saab for three A26 submarines, ~SEK 47B (~$4.8B), deliveries to 2038.

sources.title: Canada's submarine decision: Beyond Hanwha's loss in 2 minutes
sources.snippet: Canada selected Germany's TKMS over Hanwha Ocean on July 7, 2026 for the CPSP submarine program.

sources.title: South Korea's Hanwha, TKMS near Canada sub decision
sources.snippet: Canadian Patrol Submarine Project to replace Victoria-class with up to 12 subs; estimated ~60 trillion won (~$39.3B).

sources.title: Success in Canadian sub bid could propel Korea toward wins in Greece and Saudi Arabia
sources.snippet: ROK submarine prospects include Greece, Saudi Arabia, Middle East, Philippines.

sources.title: Korea Defense Industry 2026: The $37B K-Arms Export Boom
sources.snippet: Lists 2026 deals: Saudi $3.2B Cheongung-II, Poland $4B Chunmoo, Norway $922M Chunmoo, Philippines $712M FA-50; 2026 export target $37.7B, backlog >$72B.

sources.title: South Korea Joins the World's Top 10 Arms-Exporting Nations
sources.snippet: South Korea ranked ninth among world's largest arms exporters, ~3% of global market (SIPRI 2020-24).

sources.title: South Korea Refuses to Comment on UAE's Urgent Request for Cheongung-II Missiles
sources.snippet: UAE asked South Korea to expedite Cheongung-II delivery and interceptor missiles in 2026.

sources.title: Cheongung-II Destroys 29 of 30 Iranian Missiles in UAE Combat Debut
sources.snippet: Cheongung-II achieved ~96% interception during Iranian attacks on UAE (March 2026), spurring global demand.

sources.title: First customized K9PL howitzers leave South Korea for Poland under second major artillery contract
sources.snippet: Hanwha shipped first K9PL howitzers July 3, 2026; second executive contract (Dec 1, 2023) covers 152 vehicles.

key_facts: Poland's K2 tank SECOND executive contract was signed August 1, 2025 (~$6.5 billion; some cite $6.7B with support packages) — NOT a pending July–August 2026 deal. It covers 180 tanks (K2GF + localized K2PL) plus 81 support vehicles.
As of July 10, 2026, no confirmed publicly announced single ROK arms export contract worth >$5 billion is scheduled to sign in the specific July–August 2026 window.

Poland's Orka submarine program was awarded to Sweden's Saab (three A26 submarines, ~SEK 47B / ~$4.8B) on June 29, 2026 — South Korea's Hanwha Ocean LOST despite offering a free Jang Bogo-class submarine.
Canada's CPSP submarine program was awarded to Germany's TKMS (212CD) on July 7, 2026 — Hanwha Ocean/HD Hyundai LOST. Program valued ~60 trillion won (~$39.3B, up to CA$100B lifecycle).
A K2 THIRD executive contract is anticipated under the 2022 framework (up to ~1,000 tanks across ~6 contracts) but has NO confirmed date or value for mid-2026.
April 2026 Hyundai Rotem–Bumar-Łabędy agreement is a K2PL local production/maintenance cooperation pact, not a new multi-billion procurement contract.

UAE requested accelerated Cheongung-II (KM-SAM Block II) delivery and follow-on interceptors after a ~96% interception combat debut against Iranian missiles/drones (Feb–March 2026); follow-on orders under review, no confirmed new >$5B contract.
Saudi Arabia reportedly signed a ~$3.2 billion Cheongung-II contract in late 2025; Iraq also purchased KM-SAM/Cheongung-II.
Other 2026 deals are sub-$5B: Poland Chunmoo ~$4B (Dec 2025), Norway ~$922M Chunmoo (Feb 2026), Philippines $712M FA-50 (June 2025).
South Korea ranks ~9th–10th globally in arms exports (SIPRI 2020–24), 2nd-largest supplier to European NATO after the US; 2026 export target ~$37.7B, Big Four backlog >$72B through 2030.
